title: Pokopia Full Walkthrough Guide
content:
Complete walkthrough for Pokemon Pokopia covering all areas: Withered Wasteland, Bleak Beach, Rocky Ridges, and Sparkling Skylands.

WITHERED WASTELAND WALKTHROUGH:

Section 1: Befriend the Kanto Starters
- Customize your human Ditto character
- Talk to Professor Tangrowth
- Interact with gold shiny spot in wheelbarrow to obtain Pokedex
- Learn Water Gun from Squirtle
- Use Water Gun to create Tall Grass and Pretty Flower Bed habitats
- Befriend Bulbasaur and complete the "Water dry grass!" request (revive 10 Dry Tall Grass patches)
- Unlock Leafage move from Bulbasaur
- Create Tall Grass habitat for Squirtle
- Befriend Charmander by creating habitat and lighting campfire

Section 2: Build Your First Home
- Follow Tangrowth to destroyed Pokemon Center
- Create Boulder-Shaded Tall Grass to unlock Timburr
- Complete challenges at PC for Life Coins
- Craft Straw Bed (2 Leaves)
- Purchase Leaf Den Kit from Shop
- Gather 3 Sturdy Sticks and 3 Leaves for construction
- Have Timburr help build - takes real-world hours
- Place 3 furniture inside house
- Place Ditto Flag to mark as your house

Section 3: Rebuild the Pokemon Center
- Raise environment level to 3 to unlock Pokemon Center Rebuilding Kit (1000 Life Coins)
- Gather materials: 20 Lumber, 20 Stones, 10 Leaves, 10 Vine Ropes
- For Lumber: befriend Scyther (Tree-Shaded Tall Grass), give Sturdy Sticks to unlock Cut move
- Scyther can convert 2 Small Logs into 10 Lumber

Section 4: Unlock Rock Smash and Slowpoke
- Create Exercise Resting Spot habitat (Punching Bag + Seat) for Hitmonchan
- Give 2 Stones to Hitmonchan to unlock Rock Smash move
- Head west to find Drifloon (unlocks Dream Island feature)
- Find Onix beyond rocks destroyed with Rock Smash
- Create Seaside Tall Grass habitat (4 grass patches on beach) for Slowpoke
- Help Slowpoke travel to Pokemon Center using Wooden Steps
- Complete "Yawn Up a Storm!" request by creating Horsea Fountains and Rain Dance Site
- Use Castform Weather Charms for Rain Dance Site
- Get 100 humidity points, talk to Kyogre to make it rain
- Free Onix and complete the request

Section 5: Finish Rebuilding Pokemon Center
- Gather Onix and 7 other Pokemon
- Collect materials: 20 Lumber, 20 Stones, 10 Leaves, 10 Vine Ropes
- Wait until tomorrow or change Switch 2 system time

BLEAK BEACH WALKTHROUGH:

Section 1: Enter the Gate and Restore the PC
- Approach green gate east of Pokemon Center
- Follow Tangrowth to destroyed bridge
- Craft Bridge Planks at Workbench and connect bridges
- Talk to Tangrowth at destroyed Pokemon Center
- Use Beach Sand blocks to remove water
- Boot up PC

Section 2: Complete Meowth's Requests
- Talk to Meowth, follow to shack
- Place blocks behind house to stop water
- Create Tropical Vibes habitat (Seashore Flowers + Tropical Tree)
- Create Knitting Station habitat
- Create Mushroom Lamps (need Glowing Mushrooms from caves)
- Create Hydrated Yellow Tall Grass (4 Yellow Tall Grass near water) for Piplup
- Have Piplup spray bubbles on muddy areas, use Water Gun to wash 80 squares of mud
- Build Chirp-Chirp Meal habitat for Torchic
- Create Planter (1 Brick + 1 Stone) and plant flowers
- Build house with 3 furniture inside

Section 3: Unlock Surf from Piplup
- Create Trash Collection Site (Waste Bin, Garbage Bag, Sign) for Trubbish
- Give Nonburnable Garbage to Trubbish for Iron Ore
- Create Shower at workbench
- Build Breezy Flower Bed (4 Seashore Flowers) for Pawmi
- Build Tropical Seaside (1 Large Palm Tree, 2 Ocean Water blocks, 4 Hedges)
- Befriend Lapras
- Complete request to unlock Surf move

Section 4: Complete Brighten Things Up Request
- Befriend Pawmi/Pawmo, light up Harbor Streetlight
- Follow Trubbish to find moss-covered Snorlax (Mosslax)
- Need 100 bloom points
- Create Shell Lamp (1 Shell + 1 Sea Glass Fragments)
- Connect Utility Poles to wind turbine, water wheel, and furnace to generate electricity
- Connect unlit lights to utility poles
- At 100 Bloom Points, talk to Raikou at top of lighthouse

ROCKY RIDGES WALKTHROUGH:

Section 1: Enter the Gate and Restore the PC
- Head to northwest gate in Withered Wasteland
- Create Red Tall Grass habitat (2x2 red grass)
- Befriend Scorbunny and Machoke
- Unlock Strength from Machoke (hold A while moving to carry rocks)
- Push PC to electric socket

Section 2: Rescue Chef Dente
- Create Pointy Tree-Shaded Rocky Tall Grass
- Befriend Dartrix, create Perch (2 Small Logs, 1 Leaf) to get Cutting Board recipe
- Make Simple Salad (2 Leaves) - strengthens Leafage
- Create Mossy Hot Spring (4 Moss Plants near water) for Torkoal
- Create Bread Oven (2 Stones, 2 Small Logs, 4 Volcanic Ashes)
- Grow Wheat and make Simple Bread - strengthens Cut
- Cut chain to rescue Chef Dente

Section 3: Unlock Rollout from Graveler
- Create Digging and Burning habitat (Wheelbarrow + Excavation Tools + Smelting Furnace) for Magmar
- Harvest copper from ore blocks with Rock Smash
- Create Frying Pan (2 Copper Ingots)
- Grow beans, make Simple Hamburger Steaks - strengthens Rock Smash
- Create Mossy Boulder habitat for Graveler - unlock Rollout move

Section 4: Complete Time to Party! Request
- Talk to DJ Rotom
- Place Party Cooking Pot
- Need 100 mood - place relics, balloons, stage stands, party decorations
- Create Uplifting Duckweed habitat (4 Duckweed near water) for Ludicolo
- Create Party Platters and dishes
- Gather 15 Leppa Berries, 15 Wheat, 15 Beans, 15 Honey
- Wait 6 minutes for curry
- Talk to Volcanion at mountain top

SPARKLING SKYLANDS WALKTHROUGH:

Section 1: Enter the Gate and Restore the PC
- Enter Bleak Beach, head north to gate
- Get on platform machine to reach island
- Befriend Rookidee
- Create Tree-Shaded Pink Tall Grass for Sprigatito
- Create Hydrated Pink Tall Grass for Froakie

Section 2: Learn Glide from Dragonite
- Build Waterside Dinghy (1 Canoe, 2 Duckweeds) at high-up water location
- Talk to Dragonite to unlock Glide move

Section 3: Complete Rebuild the Huge Building! Request
- Meet Tinkmaster
- Create Construction-Site Generator habitat (1 Iron Pipe, 2 Iron Scaffolds) for Conkeldurr
- Create Concrete Mixer (2 Iron Ingots)
- Create Lift Platform (5 Iron Ingots, 5 Concrete, 1 Tinkagear)

Floor materials:
- 2nd floor: 25 Concrete, 10 Glass, 5 Pokemetal, 10 Iron Ingots, 6 Pokemon
- 3rd floor: 25 Concrete, 10 Glass, 10 Shiny Stones, 10 Copper Ingots
- 4th floor: 40 Concrete, 15 Glass, 10 Paper, 10 Bricks, 20 Lumber, 15 Pokemon

For Glass: Use Volcanic Ash, Sand, or Sandstone in Furnace with Burn Specialty Pokemon (Vulpix)
For Pokemetal: Mine fragments on floating island east (befriend Gardevoir for location)
For Paper: Build Professor's Apprentice Program habitat (Whiteboard, Jumbled Cords, Table, Laptop) for Metang

After fourth floor, take elevator to rooftop. Examine Master Ball to raise Trainer Rank to Master.

title: Withered Wasteland Area Guide
content:
Withered Wasteland is the first area in Pokemon Pokopia. Initially you only meet Professor Tangrowth here, but you can restore the area by befriending Pokemon, creating habitats, and building structures.

Key Features:
- Starting area of the game
- Home to Professor Tangrowth
- Location of the first Pokemon Center
- Access to Bleak Beach (east gate) and Rocky Ridges (northwest gate)

Important Locations:
- Professor Tangrowth's starting location
- Destroyed Pokemon Center (rebuildable)
- Beach area with Slowpoke habitat
- Cave where you first met Tangrowth
- Abandoned building (Team Initiation Challenge)

Key Pokemon to Befriend:
- Bulbasaur (Tall Grass habitat)
- Squirtle (Hydrated Tall Grass habitat)
- Charmander (Tall Grass habitat near campfire)
- Timburr (Boulder-Shaded Tall Grass)
- Scyther (Tree-Shaded Tall Grass) - teaches Cut
- Hitmonchan (Exercise Resting Spot) - teaches Rock Smash
- Slowpoke (Seaside Tall Grass)
- Onix
- Kyogre (legendary)

Key Requests:
- Water dry grass! (first request from Bulbasaur)
- Yawn Up a Storm! (important request with Slowpoke)
- Do the Team Initiation Challenge

Environment Level Goals:
- Level 3 to unlock Pokemon Center Rebuilding Kit
- Level 5 for endgame content

title: Bleak Beach Area Guide
content:
Bleak Beach is one of two biomes accessible after finishing the main request in Withered Wasteland. Here you meet Meowth and help restore the biome's electricity.

Key Features:
- Beach and ocean environment
- Muddy areas that can be cleaned
- Windmill for generating electricity
- Lighthouse area

Unlock Requirements:
- Complete Withered Wasteland main request
- Reach Super Trainer Rank (befriend Kyogre)

Important NPCs:
- Meowth (merchant/helper)
- Peakychu (pale Pikachu variant)
- Mosslax (moss-covered Snorlax)

Key Pokemon:
- Meowth (Resting Spot habitat)
- Piplup (Hydrated Yellow Tall Grass) - teaches Surf
- Torchic (Chirp-Chirp Meal habitat)
- Trubbish (Trash Collection Site)
- Pawmi/Pawmo (Breezy Flower Bed)
- Lapras (Tropical Seaside)
- Raikou (legendary)

Key Requests:
- Rebuild the Pokemon Center
- Brighten Things Up (Mosslax request)

Special Mechanics:
- Electricity system with Utility Poles, windmill, water wheel, and furnace
- Mud cleaning with Water Gun (80 squares needed)
- Bloom Points system for Mosslax (need 100)

title: Rocky Ridges Area Guide
content:
Rocky Ridges is one of two areas accessible after finishing Withered Wasteland. Meet Chef Dente (Greedent) and DJ Rotom here.

Key Features:
- Mountainous rocky terrain
- Cave systems with lava areas
- Hot springs
- Mining areas with copper ore

Unlock Requirements:
- Complete Withered Wasteland main request
- Reach Super Trainer Rank (befriend Kyogre)

Important NPCs:
- Chef Dente (Greedent) - teaches cooking
- DJ Rotom - plays music CDs

Key Pokemon:
- Scorbunny (Red Tall Grass)
- Machoke (Grassy Training Field) - teaches Strength
- Dartrix (Pointy Tree-Shaded Rocky Tall Grass)
- Torkoal (Mossy Hot Spring)
- Magmar (Digging and Burning habitat)
- Graveler (Mossy Boulder) - teaches Rollout
- Ludicolo (Uplifting Duckweed)
- Volcanion (legendary)

Key Requests:
- Rebuild the Pokemon Center
- Time to Party! (DJ Rotom request)

Special Items:
- Copper Ore (for Copper Ingots)
- Volcanic Ash (for Bread Oven and Glass)
- Glowing Stones (third floor of huge building)

Food Power-Ups:
- Simple Salad: Strengthens Leafage (wider area, grow moss and aquatic plants)
- Simple Bread: Strengthens Cut (cut through solid objects, chains, wire mesh)
- Simple Hamburger Steak: Strengthens Rock Smash (destroy hard rocks)

title: Sparkling Skylands Area Guide
content:
Sparkling Skylands is the fourth and final biome in Pokopia. Here you meet Dragonite (teaches Glide) and Tinkmaster (Tinkaton, helps rebuild the skyscraper).

Key Features:
- Floating islands
- High-altitude areas requiring Glide
- Huge skyscraper building to rebuild

Unlock Requirements:
- Complete both Bleak Beach AND Rocky Ridges main requests
- Befriend Raikou and Volcanion
- Reach Hyper Trainer Rank

Important NPCs:
- Tinkmaster (Tinkaton) - leads building projects

Key Pokemon:
- Rookidee (first area)
- Sprigatito (Tree-Shaded Pink Tall Grass)
- Froakie (Hydrated Pink Tall Grass)
- Dragonite (Waterside Dinghy) - teaches Glide
- Conkeldurr (Construction-Site Generator)
- Vulpix (Fluffy Flower Garden) - Burn specialty for Glass
- Metang (Professor's Apprentice Program) - converts Wastepaper to Paper
- Gardevoir (location hint for Pokemetal)

Key Requests:
- Rebuild the Huge Building!

Building Materials:
- 2nd floor: 25 Concrete, 10 Glass, 5 Pokemetal, 10 Iron Ingots, 6 Pokemon
- 3rd floor: 25 Concrete, 10 Glass, 10 Shiny Stones, 10 Copper Ingots
- 4th floor: 40 Concrete, 15 Glass, 10 Paper, 10 Bricks, 20 Lumber, 15 Pokemon

Special Items:
- Tinkagears (from Tinkmaster)
- Limestone (for Concrete)
- Pokemetal fragments (floating island east)

Endgame:
- After 4th floor, take elevator to rooftop
- Examine Master Ball to raise Trainer Rank to Master

title: List of All Moves
content:
Moves are abilities Ditto learns from befriending Pokemon. Use moves to transform the land, create habitats, and progress through the game.

EARLY GAME MOVES:

Water Gun (from Squirtle)
- Create Tall Grass habitats
- Create Pretty Flower Bed habitats
- Revive dead trees
- Clean mud (Bleak Beach)
- Create water blocks on lava (Sparkling Skylands)

Leafage (from Bulbasaur)
- Create Tall Grass habitats
- Create flower habitats
- Power-up with Simple Salad: wider area, grow moss and aquatic plants

Cut (from Scyther)
- Chop down trees for Small Logs
- Convert Small Logs to Lumber (2 Logs = 10 Lumber)
- Power-up with Simple Bread: cut through solid objects, chains, wire mesh

Rock Smash (from Hitmonchan)
- Destroy rocks blocking paths
- Reveal water sources from shiny rocks
- Harvest copper from ore blocks
- Power-up with Simple Hamburger Steak: destroy hard rocks and Glowing Stones

MID GAME MOVES:

Strength (from Machoke)
- Hold A while moving to carry giant rocks
- Push objects including PC

Surf (from Piplup/Lapras)
- Swim freely in water
- Access new areas

Rollout (from Graveler)
- Destroy blocks and obstacles

LATE GAME MOVES:

Glide (from Dragonite)
- Fly between floating islands
- Access high-altitude areas
- Consumes PP

Magnet Rise (from Magnemite - postgame)
- Hover and fly over terrain
- Unlocked after completing Team Initiation Challenge
- Create Factory Storage habitat: Streetlight, Metal Drum, Jumbled Cord, Control Unit

MOVE POWER-UPS:

Eat specific foods to strengthen moves:
- Simple Salad (2 Leaves) → Strengthens Leafage
- Simple Bread (3 Wheat) → Strengthens Cut
- Simple Hamburger Steak (3 Beans) → Strengthens Rock Smash

Note: Food power-ups have limited uses - make lots of them!

PP RECOVERY:
- Eat food such as berries to recover PP
- Blue curved bar at bottom right shows PP meter
- Running out of PP makes Ditto tired when using moves

title: List of All Materials
content:
Materials are resources used for crafting items and building structures in Pokemon Pokopia.

BASIC MATERIALS:

Leaves
- Found on ground near trees
- Used for Straw Bed (2), Simple Salad (2)

Sturdy Sticks
- Gathered from ground
- Used for Campfire (1), Leaf Den construction (3)

Small Logs
- Obtained by cutting trees with Cut move
- Convert to Lumber via Scyther (2 Logs = 10 Lumber)

Lumber
- Processed from Small Logs
- Used for Storage Box (1), many furniture items

Stones
- Found on ground
- Used for Campfire (1), Workbench (2), Rock Smash unlock

Vine Ropes
- Used for Pokemon Center construction

CRAFTING MATERIALS:

Bricks
- Made from Squishy Clay (processed by Torchic)
- Used for Planter (1 Brick + 1 Stone)

Copper Ore
- Found in Rocky Ridges caves
- Harvest with Rock Smash
- Smelt into Copper Ingots

Copper Ingots
- Made from Copper Ore in Smelting Furnace
- Used for Frying Pan (2), Concrete Mixer (2)

Iron Ore
- Obtained by giving Nonburnable Garbage to Trubbish
- Found in Rocky Ridges caves
- Smelt into Iron Ingots

Iron Ingots
- Made from Iron Ore
- Used for Lift Platform (5), Utility Poles, many building items

Limestone
- Found in Sparkling Skylands
- Convert to Concrete using Concrete Mixer with Conkeldurr

Concrete
- Made from Limestone
- Used for Huge Building construction (25+ per floor)

Glass
- Made from Volcanic Ash, Sand, or Sandstone in Furnace
- Requires Burn specialty Pokemon (Vulpix)
- Used for Huge Building construction

Pokemetal
- Mine fragments on floating island east (Sparkling Skylands)
- Befriend Gardevoir for location hint
- Used for Huge Building 2nd floor (5)

Shiny Stones
- Found in Rocky Ridges cave (where you met Magmar)
- Follow train tracks to concrete pillars with Poke Ball logo
- Rock Smash blocks, eat Simple Hamburger Steak to destroy
- Used for Huge Building 3rd floor (10)

Paper
- Convert Wastepaper using Metang
- Build Professor's Apprentice Program habitat
- Used for Huge Building 4th floor (10)

FOOD MATERIALS:

Leppa Berries
- Found by headbutting trees
- Used for Rain Dance Site, Party curry

Wheat
- Grow at fields (Rocky Ridges southwest)
- Destroy rocks blocking water
- Used for Simple Bread (3), Party curry (15)

Beans
- Grow at fields (Rocky Ridges southwest)
- Used for Simple Hamburger Steak (3), Party curry (15)

Honey
- Collected from glowing flowers
- Used for Party curry (15)

SPECIAL MATERIALS:

Volcanic Ash
- Found in black soil (Rocky Ridges east)
- Used for Bread Oven (4), Glass making

Squishy Clay
- Found in clay blocks
- Use Rock Smash to harvest
- Process into Bricks via Torchic

Glowing Mushrooms
- Found in caves
- Used for Mushroom Lamp

Cotton Spores
- Event item (More Spores for Hoppip event)

Red Crystal Fragments
- Mined in Dream Islands
- Sableye event currency

title: List of Pokemon by Specialties
content:
Each Pokemon has at least one specialty that allows them to help with rebuilding the island.

SPECIALTY CATEGORIES:

Construction & Building:
- Builder: Helps construct buildings
- Chop: Cuts trees for lumber (Scyther)
- Engineer: Leads large building projects (Tinkmaster)

Resource Processing:
- Burn: Burns materials in furnace for Glass (Vulpix)
- Process: Converts raw materials (Torchic - Squishy Clay to Bricks)
- Paper: Converts Wastepaper to Paper (Metang)

Exploration & Discovery:
- Appraise: Identifies lost relics (Professor Tangrowth)
- Search: Locates hidden items with Dowsing Machine
- Illuminate: Lights up areas (Peakychu)

Utility:
- DJ: Plays music CDs (DJ Rotom)
- Party: Helps prepare food for parties (Chef Dente)
- Paint: Customizes furniture colors (Smearguru)
- Eat: Devours food for buffs (Mosslax)

HOW TO USE SPECIALTIES:

1. Check Pokedex to see what specialties a Pokemon has
2. Talk to Pokemon or press Up on D-pad to have them follow you
3. Lead them to the item or location needing their specialty
4. They will automatically help when nearby

KEY SPECIALTY POKEMON:

Professor Tangrowth - Appraise
- Shows what kind of lost relic you've found
- Habitat: Professor's treasure trove (Bed + 4 Lost Relics)

DJ Rotom - DJ
- Plays music CDs for you
- Habitat: Music and magazines (CD Rack, Magazine Rack, CD Player)
- Location: Rocky Ridges

Chef Dente - Party
- Helps prepare food for parties
- Habitat: Chef's kitchen (Plain Table, Modern Sink, Cooking Stove, Cutting Board, Stylish Cooking Pot, Plated food)
- Location: Rocky Ridges

Smearguru - Paint
- Changes color of furniture and items
- Habitat: Tiny atelier (Seat + Canvas)
- Location: Bleak Beach

Peakychu - Illuminate
- Emits light to illuminate the whole town
- Habitat: Full recovery (Plain Chest, First Aid Kit, Bed)
- Location: Bleak Beach

Mosslax - Eat
- Devours food offered on Offering Dish
- Provides buffs based on food's flavor or rarity
- Habitat: Gourmet's altar (Offering dish)
- Location: Bleak Beach

Tinkmaster - Engineer
- Leads other Pokemon during large building projects
- Habitat: Oversized dumping ground (Tires, 3 Iron beams/columns, Waste bin, Microwave oven)
- Location: Sparkling Skylands

title: List of All Requests
content:
Requests are side quests given by Pokemon after befriending them. Complete requests to unlock new moves, recipes, and story progression.

IMPORTANT REQUESTS (Story-Critical):

Yawn Up a Storm! (Withered Wasteland)
- Given by Slowpoke
- Create Horsea Fountains and Rain Dance Site
- Get 100 humidity points
- Talk to Kyogre to make it rain
- Reward: Story progression

Brighten Things Up (Bleak Beach)
- Given by Peakychu
- Light up the area
- Connect electricity via Utility Poles
- Get 100 Bloom Points
- Talk to Raikou at lighthouse top
- Reward: Story progression

Time to Party! (Rocky Ridges)
- Given by DJ Rotom
- Place Party Cooking Pot
- Get 100 mood with decorations
- Create Uplifting Duckweed for Ludicolo
- Make Party Platters and dishes
- Gather ingredients for curry
- Reward: Talk to Volcanion

Rebuild the Huge Building! (Sparkling Skylands)
- Given by Tinkmaster
- Gather materials for each floor:
  - 2nd: 25 Concrete, 10 Glass, 5 Pokemetal, 10 Iron Ingots, 6 Pokemon
  - 3rd: 25 Concrete, 10 Glass, 10 Shiny Stones, 10 Copper Ingots
  - 4th: 40 Concrete, 15 Glass, 10 Paper, 10 Bricks, 20 Lumber, 15 Pokemon
- Reward: Master Trainer Rank

Do the Team Initiation Challenge
- Unlocks early but requires progress across all areas
- Postgame: Befriend Magnemite, learn Magnet Rise

Rebuild the Pokemon Center (Each Area)
- Withered Wasteland: 20 Lumber, 20 Stones, 10 Leaves, 10 Vine Ropes, 8 Pokemon
- Bleak Beach: Similar materials
- Rocky Ridges: Similar materials

EARLY REQUESTS:

Water dry grass! (Bulbasaur)
- Use Water Gun to revive 10 patches of Dry Tall Grass
- Reward: Leafage move

HOW TO FIND REQUESTS:

1. Check Pokedex menu for all active requests
2. Look for Pokemon with yellow speech bubbles (subtle yellow outline)
3. Talk to Professor Tangrowth for reminders on current requests

REQUEST TIPS:

- Requests unlock new moves and recipes
- Some requests are required for story progression
- Talk to Pokemon after befriending to receive requests
- Check speech bubble color - yellow indicates story relevance

title: List of Unique Pokemon NPCs
content:
Special Pokemon serve as Unique NPCs in Pokemon Pokopia, providing help as you progress through the story.

UNIQUE NPCs:

Professor Tangrowth
- Role: Pokemon Professor of Pokopia
- Used to live with a human professor (owns the glasses on its chest)
- Guides you at game start
- First found in: Withered Wasteland
- Specialty: Appraise (identifies lost relics)
- Ideal Habitat: Humid
- Likes: Nature, complicated stuff, letters/words, strange stuff, group activities, dry flavors
- Habitat: Professor's treasure trove (Bed + 4 Lost Relics)

DJ Rotom
- Role: Changes in-game music
- Give CDs to unlock new music tracks
- First found in: Rocky Ridges
- Specialty: DJ (plays music CDs)
- Ideal Habitat: Bright
- Likes: Shiny stuff, glass stuff, noisy stuff, group activities, blocky stuff, bitter flavors
- Habitat: Music and magazines (CD Rack, Magazine Rack, CD Player)

Chef Dente (Greedent)
- Role: Teaches cooking
- Makes meals to power up moves
- First found in: Rocky Ridges
- Specialty: Party (helps prepare food for parties)
- Ideal Habitat: Warm
- Likes: Soft stuff, metal stuff, group activities, looks like food, luxury, sweet flavors
- Habitat: Chef's kitchen (Plain Table, Modern Sink, Cooking Stove, Cutting Board, Stylish Cooking Pot, Plated food)

Smearguru (Smeargle)
- Role: Customizes furniture colors
- Uses Paint move on eligible items
- First found in: Bleak Beach
- Specialty: Paint (changes item colors and patterns)
- Ideal Habitat: Bright
- Likes: Soft stuff, fabric, strange stuff, colorful stuff, shiny stuff, dry flavors
- Habitat: Tiny atelier (Seat + Canvas)

Peakychu (Pale Pikachu variant)
- Role: Illuminates the town
- Gives Brighten Things Up request
- First found in: Bleak Beach
- Specialty: Illuminate (emits light to illuminate whole town)
- Ideal Habitat: Bright
- Likes: Electronics, glass stuff, cute stuff, healing, letters/words, sweet flavors
- Habitat: Full recovery (Plain Chest, First Aid Kit, Bed)

Mosslax (Moss-covered Snorlax)
- Role: Provides buffs for food offerings
- Devours food on Offering Dish
- First found in: Bleak Beach (after finding Peakychu)
- Specialty: Eat (provides buffs based on food flavor/rarity)
- Ideal Habitat: Bright
- Likes: Soft stuff, fabric, looks like food, watching stuff, strange stuff, spicy flavors
- Habitat: Gourmet's altar (Offering dish)

Tinkmaster (Tinkaton)
- Role: Leads large building projects
- Helps rebuild the huge building
- First found in: Sparkling Skylands (after learning Glide)
- Specialty: Engineer (leads other Pokemon during large building/rebuilding projects)
- Ideal Habitat: Bright
- Likes: Cute stuff, metal stuff, construction, blocky stuff, exercise, spicy flavors
- Habitat: Oversized dumping ground (Tires, 3 Iron beams/columns, Waste bin, Microwave oven)

title: List of Areas and Biomes
content:
Biomes are the main areas in Pokemon Pokopia where you can encounter Pokemon and build habitats and buildings.

ALL BIOMES:

1. Withered Wasteland
- First area of the game
- Initially only Professor Tangrowth here
- Restore by befriending Pokemon, creating habitats, building structures
- Gates to Bleak Beach (east) and Rocky Ridges (northwest)

2. Bleak Beach
- One of two biomes unlocked after Withered Wasteland
- Beach and ocean environment
- Meet Meowth here
- Help restore electricity to brighten the area

3. Rocky Ridges
- One of two areas unlocked after Withered Wasteland
- Meet Chef Dente and DJ Rotom here
- Mountainous terrain with caves and hot springs

4. Sparkling Skylands
- Fourth and final biome
- Meet Dragonite (teaches Glide) and Tinkmaster
- Floating islands

- Total of comfort levels of all Pokemon in the area

title: Beginner's Guide to Pokopia: Tips and Tricks
content:
Tips and tricks for new players starting Pokemon Pokopia.

BEGINNER TIPS:

1. Inspect Sparkles for Habitat Hints
- Sparkles near objects give habitat creation hints
- Sparkling spots in water may contain items
- Always check unusual spots

2. Pokopia Follows Real-Time Clock
- Game matches your Nintendo Switch 2 system time
- Daytime in real life = daytime in-game
- Affects Pokemon spawning (time of day requirements)
- Construction projects complete at specific times

3. Hold Y to Inhale Items
- Pick up items individually with Y
- Hold Y to inhale multiple loose items at once
- Saves time when gathering

4. Eat Food to Replenish PP
- PP meter is blue curved bar (bottom right)
- Running out of PP makes Ditto tired
- Eat berries and food to recover PP

5. Utilize Pokemon Specialties
- Each Pokemon has at least one specialty
- Check Pokedex for specialty info
- Have Pokemon follow you (Up on D-pad)
- Lead them to items/locations needing their help

6. Increase Pokemon Comfort Levels
- Ask Pokemon "How's your comfort level?"
- They'll give hints for what they want
- Place appropriate items in their habitat
- Comfort levels affect Environment Level

7. Construct Buildings
- Use building kits or build block-by-block
- Buildings need at least 4 walls and 2 blocks height
- Place 3+ furniture inside to make it a home
- Ask Pokemon to follow, reassign house as new habitat

8. Place Storage Box Next to Workbench
- Draw materials from storage when crafting
- Add materials to storage beforehand
- Saves inventory space

9. Power Items with Electricity
- Lamps and lights require electricity
- Generate from Electric-type Pokemon or windmills
- Distribute through power lines (Utility Poles)
- Plan electrical grid for organized distribution

title: How to Increase Comfort Level
content:
Comfort level shows how much a Pokemon likes their home in Pokemon Pokopia.

HOW TO INCREASE COMFORT LEVEL:

1. Add Furniture to Habitat
- Simplest way is adding furniture and decorations
- Pokemon must like the additions for level to increase
- Limited by space in original habitat

2. Move Pokemon to a House
- Comfort level increases faster in a private house
- More room for decorations
- Better customization per Pokemon
- If Pokemon dislikes an item in house, they refuse to move in - switch items first

3. Decorate According to Preferences
- Check Pokedex for favorites and ideal habitats
- Decorate with items matching their preferences

4. Ask About Comfort Level
- Talk to Pokemon and ask 'How's your comfort level?'
- Get hints on what to improve

5. Complete Requests
- Completing Pokemon side quests raises their comfort level

6. Give Gifts
- Take out and hold items from inventory
- Give items to Pokemon friends
- Check favorites in Pokedex

7. Play Games
- Some Pokemon offer minigames when spoken to
- Jump Rope with Bulbasaur
- Comfort level increases after playing

COMFORT LEVEL TIERS:

1. No home
2. Iffy
3. Average
4. Nice
5. Great
6. Awesome

HOW TO CHECK:

- Talk to Pokemon and ask 'How's your comfort level?'
- Check all at once from Environment Level screen at Pokemon Center PC

WHAT COMFORT LEVEL AFFECTS:

- Combined comfort levels = Environment Level of the area
- Higher Environment Level unlocks new Challenges and shop items
- More varieties of habitats available with higher level
- Allows you to meet more Pokemon

title: How to Raise Environment Level
content:
Environment Level is the total of comfort levels of all Pokemon in an area.

MAXIMUM LEVEL: 10
REQUIRED FOR ENDGAME: Level 5 in each area

HOW TO RAISE ENVIRONMENT LEVEL:

1. Befriend More Pokemon
- Build habitats to spawn different Pokemon
- Move Pokemon to houses immediately to free habitat for next Pokemon

2. Raise Comfort Levels
- Decorate habitats and houses to match Pokemon preferences
- House Pokemon with similar preferences together
- Check habitat range by pressing Right Stick

3. Move Pokemon into Houses
- Fastest method
- Comfort level increases faster in houses
- More room for decorations

4. Complete Requests
- Raises comfort level of requesters
- Unlocks new moves, recipes, items, decorations

5. Play Mini Games
- Jump Rope, Hide-and-Seek, and quizzes
- Comfort increases even if you don't do well

ENVIRONMENT LEVEL REWARDS:

- New shop items at PC
- New Challenges (more ways to earn Life Coins)
- New habitat opportunities

IMPORTANT NOTES:

- Level can DECREASE if comfort levels drop
- Locked shop items become unavailable again if level decreases
- Shop items shared across all Pokemon Center PCs
- Reach Level 5 in each area to finish the main story

title: Time Traveling Guide: How to Change the Time of Day
content:
Adjust the Nintendo Switch 2 Date and Time to change time in Pokemon Pokopia.

HOW TO TIME TRAVEL FORWARD:

1. Save your game
2. Open System Settings from HOME screen (no need to close game)
3. Go to Date and Time under System
4. Turn OFF 'Synchronize Clock Using the Internet'
5. Manually adjust Date and Time to desired future date
6. New day starts at 5 AM - travel to 5 AM for next day
7. Start/return to Pokemon Pokopia

HOW TO TIME TRAVEL BACKWARD:

1. Save your game
2. Open System Settings from HOME screen
3. Go to Date and Time
4. Turn OFF 'Synchronize Clock Using the Internet'
5. Travel to at least one day BEFORE your desired date
6. Open game briefly to process change
7. Save and close
8. Go back to settings and time travel forward to desired date

PENALTIES: None! No penalties for time traveling.

WHAT TO USE TIME TRAVEL FOR:

- Skip construction waiting times
- Encounter time-exclusive Pokemon
- Reset daily activities (5 AM = new day)
- Complete events you missed
- Encounter rare Pokemon

TIME OF DAY SCHEDULE:

- Morning: 6 AM to 9 AM
- Day: 9 AM to 5 PM
- Dusk: 5 PM to 7 PM
- Night: 7 PM to 6 AM

NIGHTTIME-EXCLUSIVE POKEMON:

- Oddish
- Venonat / Venomoth
- Hoothoot / Noctowl
- Cacturne
- Zubat / Golbat / Crobat
- Gastly / Haunter / Gengar
- Cleffa / Clefairy / Clefable
- Murkrow / Honchkrow
- Misdreavus / Mismagius
- Duskull / Dusclops / Dusknoir
- Umbreon

No Pokemon are exclusive to daytime or other times of day.

CONSTRUCTION TIMES:

- Small structures (Leaf Den, Windmills): 15 minutes
- Large structures (Cottages, Pokemon Centers): Full day
- Time travel to 5 AM to skip construction times

TUTORIAL NOTE: Time is fixed during tutorial. Syncs with real time after Ditto falls asleep.

title: All Daily Activities
content:
Daily activities in Pokemon Pokopia reset every day at 5:00 AM.

DAILY ACTIVITIES:

1. Complete Daily Challenges
- Simple tasks that reset every day
- Reward Life Coins when completed
- Check at PC in Pokemon Center
- Daily Challenges refresh at 5:00 AM

2. Buy From the Daily Shop
- Rotating selection of items changes daily
- May include furniture, crafting recipes, building kits
- Check PC regularly for new items

3. Visit Dream Island
- Special area accessible once per day
- Explore unique island, gather materials, find rare items
- Show Drifloon a doll to visit
- Island type depends on doll shown
- Can revisit same island multiple times same day

4. Restore PP at the Pokemon Center
- Fully restore PP once per day using healing machine
- Saves food/berries for PP recovery

5. Obtain Furniture from Vespiquen
- 4 furniture items per day
- Exchange: 3 Honey jars per furniture piece
- Items have special patterns
- Befriend Vespiquen first

6. Offer Mosslax Food
- One offering per day
- Different flavors = different effects
- Effects last until next offering

7. Get Drinks From Every Vending Machine
- Each powered vending machine gives one random drink per day
- Drinks create water/lava or used for cooking/crafting
- Note: Won't give drink after being picked up and replaced

DAY RESET:

- New day starts at 5:00 AM system time
- Change Switch 2 system clock to 5 AM to trigger new day early
- Time traveling forward = new daily activities

title: Money Farming Guide: How to Earn Life Coins
content:
Life Coins are the main currency in Pokemon Pokopia. Use them to buy furniture, recipes, seeds, and kits.

HOW TO EARN LIFE COINS:

1. Complete Challenges
- Main method of earning Life Coins
- Unlocked when discovering PC in Withering Wasteland
- Check PC at Pokemon Center for available challenges
- Includes regular and daily tasks
- Daily Challenges reset at 5:00 AM
- Return to PC to claim coins (NOT automatic)

To maximize Challenges:
- Raise Environment Level to unlock more Challenges
- Befriend new Pokemon
- Build houses
- Complete requests

2. Complete Stamp Rally
- Every Friday: get stamp card from PC
- Fill card with 5 stamps
- Stamps from any PC (main world, other players' worlds, Cloud Island)
- Complete card before stamp period ends
- Exchange period: redeem Life Coins
- Rare stamps = more Life Coins

WHAT LIFE COINS ARE USED FOR:
- Furniture and decorations from Shop
- Crafting recipes
- Seeds for growing crops
- Building kits
- Pokemon Center Rebuilding Kit (1000 Life Coins)

SHOP ACCESS:
- Access through Pokemon Center PC
- Items unlock based on Environment Level
- Items shared across all PCs in world

title: Cloud Island Guide
content:
Cloud Islands are separate multiplayer worlds where you can build with friends.

HOW TO UNLOCK:

- Raise Environment Level to 2 to unlock Link Play feature

REQUIREMENTS:
- Online play only
- Nintendo Switch Online membership
- Stable internet connection
- Copy of game on each console

HOW TO PLAY:

Hosting:
- Create Cloud Island and invite friends via Link Code
- Control settings and name
- Only one Cloud Island at a time
- Delete existing one in Settings before creating new

Joining:
- Join through another player's Cloud Island address
- Can join even if host isn't currently playing

WHAT YOU CAN DO:

Shop:
- Purchase items from Cloud Island PC
- Different catalogue from main game
- Bought items stay in Cloud Island (can't transfer to main game)
- Life Coins are shared between main game and Cloud Island
- Recipes learned carry over to main game!

Mini Games:
- Jump Rope with friends
- Hide-and-Seek with friends
- Need Pokemon associated with mini games in Cloud Island

Photos:
- Decorate Cloud Island for group photos
- Pose and change expressions

CLOUD ISLAND CHARACTERISTICS:

- Separate world from main game
- Start from scratch (no pre-existing story progress)
- Separate inventory (can't bring main game items)
- Exception: Recipes permanently kept when learned
- Layout can be rerolled when recreating
- No facilities installed - build yourself

POKEMON:
- Main game Pokemon may ask to join you in Cloud Island
- Can use their Specialties for convenience

NOTE: Events CANNOT be completed on Cloud Islands, even with fully-built Pokemon Center.

title: Best Mosslax Offerings Guide
content:
Best foods to offer Mosslax and their effects in Pokemon Pokopia.

WHERE TO FIND MOSSLAX:
- Cave northeast of Bleak Beach
- Unlocked after completing Brighten Things Up request

HOW TO UNLOCK MOSSLAX OFFERINGS:
1. Complete the Brighten Things Up request to wake Mosslax
2. Wait for Mosslax to give you a request
3. Place plate and food in front of Mosslax
4. Complete the quest - then offer food every day

OFFERING FREQUENCY: Once per day (use time travel for more)

FOOD FLAVOR EFFECTS:

No Flavor: Able to grow closer with other Pokemon
Bitter: More likely to find rare items
Spicy: Pokemon are more likely to appear in habitats
Dry: More likely to see Pokemon with rare feathers (Rainbow/Silver Feather)
Sour: Shop might be more likely to have good items
Sweet: Easier to find ancient artifacts

FOOD EFFECTIVENESS:
- Raw Ingredient: Weak effect
- Simple Cooked Food: Medium effect
- Unique Cooked Food: Strong effect

RECOMMENDED RECIPES:

For Dry Effect (Rare Feathers):
- Crushed-berry salad: Leaf x1 + Chesto Berry x1 + Crush Pokemon
- Mushroom soup: Fresh Water x1 + Cave Mushrooms x1 + Any ingredient

For Spicy Effect (Pokemon in habitats):
- Crouton salad: Leaf x1 + Any bread
- Bread bowl: Wheat x1 + Any soup + Any berry (Burn Pokemon needed)

STRONG EFFECTS (Unique Cooked Food):

Spicy: Crouton salad
Dry: Crushed-berry salad
No flavor: Vibrant hamburger steak (Bean + Potato + Any Salad + Any ingredient)
Sour: Flavorful soup (Fresh Water + Aspear Berry + Any Hamburger Steak)

BEST FOR FEATHERS:
- Crushed-berry salad (dry flavor)
- Causes Rainbow Feathers and Silver Feathers to spawn more easily
- Trade with Gimmighoul for furniture

title: Dream Island Guide: How to Visit Dream Islands
content:
Dream Islands are special areas you can visit once per day to find rare items.

HOW TO UNLOCK:
1. Find Drifloon in Withered Wasteland (west side, beach near bridge to Palette Town)
2. Create Drifloon's Riding Warm Updrafts habitat
3. Have any doll in inventory
4. Show doll to Drifloon to visit

VISITING RULES:
- One Dream Island per day
- Can revisit same island multiple times within the day
- Same doll = same island for the day
- Only Drifloon has the Dream Island specialty

DREAM ISLAND TYPES:

Wasteland Dream Island
- Required Doll: Eevee Doll
- Materials: Leppa Berry, Vine Rope, Glowing Mushrooms

Ocean Dream Island
- Required Doll: Pikachu Doll
- Materials: Twine, Sea Glass Fragments, Seashell
- Legendary: Raikou

Rock Peak Dream Island
- Required Doll: Clefairy Doll
- Materials: Cave Mushrooms, Copper Ore, Limestone

Volcanic Dream Island
- Required Doll: Arcanine Doll
- Materials: Iron Ore, Gold Ore, Glowing Stone
- Legendary: Entei

Sky Dream Island

- Legendary: Mewtwo

Random Dream Island (Wasteland, Ocean, Rock, Volcanic, OR Sky)

WHAT TO FIND ON DREAM ISLANDS:
- Stardust (important for multiplayer)
- Relics, Fossils, and CDs

title: How to GameShare Pokopia
content:
GameShare lets you play Pokopia with friends who don't own the game.

HOW TO UNLOCK GAMESHARE:
1. Unlock Palette Town
2. Boot up Palette Town PC
3. Befriend Eevee
4. GameShare and multiplayer features unlock

GAMESHARE IS ONLY AVAILABLE IN PALETTE TOWN
(No other area allows GameShare sessions)

LOCAL GAMESHARE (Works with Switch 1 OR Switch 2):

1. Host: Go to Palette Town PC → Link Play → Invite Others to Visit → GameShare
2. Host: Choose start with nearby players
3. Nearby players: Select GameShare on their Switch menu → Join GameShare
4. Host: Select Start to begin
5. Each player controls a randomized Ditto character
6. Host ends session with Close the Connection

ONLINE GAMESHARE (Switch 2 only, requires GameChat):

1. Host: Create GameChat room (1-3 friends), all participants need GameChat set up
2. Host: Palette Town PC → Link Play → Invite Others → GameShare via GameChat
3. Other players: Join via GameChat room
4. Each player controls randomized Ditto
5. End: Host uses Close the Connection, or players exit via GameChat

GAMECHAT NOTE: Free initially, but from March 31, 2026 requires Nintendo Switch Online membership.

GAMESHARE FEATURES:
- Players share host's moves and emotes
- Go Home teleports to host's location
- Players have own inventories (capacity = host's inventory slots)
- Items after session stored in Lost and Found box at Palette Town Pokemon Center

GAMESHARE LIMITATIONS:
- Cannot customize character
- Cannot build, craft, or use PC
- Cannot enter houses
- Cannot examine Pokemon Traces
- Cannot deposit building materials
- Cannot ask other Pokemon to follow them
- Cannot open Lost and Found box
- Mini games disabled during GameShare
- Can still destroy and place blocks
- Best for block-by-block building with host

title: Do the Team Initiation Challenge Walkthrough
content:
Complete walkthrough for the Team Initiation Challenge important request.

Requester: Professor Tangrowth
Location: Withered Wasteland
Completion: Required for postgame access

STEP-BY-STEP OBJECTIVES:

Step 1: Gather 5 Leppa Berries
- Reward: Bouldery Badge
- Find Leppa Berry trees (especially in Withered Wasteland)
- Water dry trees with Water Gun
- Headbutt trees to collect berries

Step 2: Gather 10 Beans, 10 Tomatoes, and 10 Wheat
- Reward: Cascade-like Badge
- Beans: Grow in Withered Wasteland
- Tomatoes: Grow in Bleak Beach
- Wheat: Grow in Rocky Ridges
- Use Rototiller to plant, Grow specialty Pokemon to speed growth

Step 3: Gather 20 Lumber, 5 Fluff, and 10 Paper
- Reward: Thunderish Badge
- Lumber: Cut trees for Small Logs → give to Scyther (Chop specialty)
- Paper: Give Wastepaper to Trubbish (Recycle specialty)
- Fluff: Litter specialty Pokemon (Mareep, Jumpluff) drop it around habitat/house

Step 4: Make 40 Bricks, 20 Gold Ingots, and 50 Concrete
- Reward: Rainbowish Badge
- Bricks: Rock Smash clay blocks for Squishy Clay → give to Torchic (Burn) OR Paldean Wooper/Clodsire drops it
- Gold Ingots: Find Gold Ore in Rocky Ridges, smelt in Furnace. Also from Arcanine Doll Dream Island
- Concrete: Unlock Sparkling Skylands, use Concrete Mixer with Limestone and Crush specialty (Onix)

Step 5: Generate 50 Electricity, Gather 10 Crystal Fragments and 5 Tinkagears
- Reward: Soul-like Badge
- Electricity: Build 2 electric sources (Furnace + Windmill). Each source generates 30.
- Crystal Fragments: Rocky Ridges mines
- Tinkagears: Ask Tinkmaster (Sparkling Skylands) to convert Iron Ingots
- Iron Ingots: Smelt Iron Ore (give Nonburnable Garbage to Recycle Pokemon, or break Rusty Blocks)

Step 6: Craft 4 Industrial Beds, 4 Resort Lights, and 4 Office Desks
- Reward: Marshy Badge
- Industrial Bed: Iron Ingot x2 + Fluff x2 + Lumber x2
- Resort Light: Iron Ore x1 + Sea Glass Fragments x1 + Vine Rope x1
- Office Desk: Pokemetal x2

Step 7: Obtain a Refrigerator, Game Boy System, and a Washing Machine
- Reward: Volcanoey Badge
- All crafted from Pokemetal
- Refrigerator: Pokemetal x2
- Washing Machine: Pokemetal x1
- Game Boy System: Pokemetal x1
- Note: Can also get these as rewards for raising Environment Level to 5 in Bleak Beach, Rocky Ridges, and Sparkling Skylands

Step 8: Submit your Favorite Photo
- Reward: Earth Badge
- Submit any photo taken with camera
- Can use any photo from your album

Step 9: Get Party Poppers
- Reward: Memories Photo Frame
- Materials: Paper x1 + Vine Rope x1
- Get recipe from raising Environment Level to 5 in Withered Wasteland, or buy from PC

COMPLETING THE CHALLENGE:
- After completing all 9 steps and earning all 8 badges, the ending credits will roll
- This transitions the game to postgame mode

title: How to Unlock Palette Town
content:
Guide to unlocking and accessing Palette Town in Pokemon Pokopia.

HOW TO UNLOCK:

1. Learn Rock Smash
- Befriend Hitmonchan by creating their habitat
- Complete Hitmonchan's request

2. Break Blocks West of Pokemon Center
- Go west of Withered Wasteland Pokemon Center
- Find stack of blocks
- Use Rock Smash to destroy them

3. Build a Bridge
- Head forward until you reach the sea
- Turn left until you see broken bridge
- Bring blocks from inventory
- Build a bridge to cross the gap

4. Enter Palette Town
- Warning will appear: recommended to gain more game knowledge before proceeding
- Can choose to go forward regardless

5. Boot Up Palette Town PC and Befriend Eevee
- Activates GameShare and other multiplayer features
- Rename the town during this process

WHAT YOU CAN DO IN PALETTE TON:

Build and Terraform:
- Freest building area in the game
- No important requests to worry about
- Blank palette for creativity
- Build habitats for Eeveelutions and other Pokemon

GameShare (Exclusive to Palette Town):
- Play with friends who don't own the game
- Works with Switch 1 (locally) or Switch 2 (online via GameChat)

Get Eeveelutions:
- Eevee gives request to find its friends
- Complete Special Challenges to unlock Eeveelution habitats
- Complete all to befriend all 8 Eeveelutions

Legendary Birds:
- Build using Freezing Chambers Kit (Articuno)
- Build using Abandoned Power Plant Kit (Zapdos)
- Build using Altar of Flame Kit (Moltres)
- Befriend all 3 to craft Sea Bell and summon Lugia

LORE NOTE:
- Pokopia is set in a post-apocalyptic Kanto Region
- Palette Town may be the same as Pallet Town from original games
- Only area in main game not connected to main story

title: Pokopia Ending Explained
content:
SPOILER WARNING - Explanation of Pokopia's story and ending.

THE ENDING:

After completing the Team Initiation Challenge and earning all badges:
1. Voice from the lighthouse (in Withered Wasteland) asks for 2 Party Poppers
2. After submitting Party Poppers, lighthouse doesn't respond
3. Professor Tangrowth suggests firing off the party poppers
4. Party poppers trigger the lighthouse's emergency protocol
5. System thinks it's under attack - reveals itself as a ROCKET
6. Lighthouse blasts off into space!

WHAT THE CREDITS SHOW:
- The rocket finds a space pod with a Ditto plush inside
- Someone in a space suit comes out to receive the contents
- This is likely Ditto's trainer (still alive and well!)

THE MYSTERY OF THE LIGHTHOUSE:
- Voice inside is the Team R Grunt (journals found throughout game)
- Team R = Team Rocket from original Kanto/Johto games
- Team R Grunt's journal #3: Tasked with gathering resources/furniture
- Team R Grunt's journal #8: Team R were arrested by International Police
- They were arrested before recording their final lighthouse message

WHERE ARE THE HUMANS?
- Humans evacuated to SPACE
- Stored their Pokemon in PC box system for safekeeping
- Hacker's Confessions diary entries reveal the truth
- Final entry (eastern floating island, Sparkling Skylands): Hacker programmed release for Pokemon if humans don't return
- Pokemon are released into habitable areas

WHAT HAPPENED TO THE WORLD?
- Natural disasters made world uninhabitable
- Droughts, hurricanes, and volcanic eruptions
- All areas you develop show signs of this destruction

SECRET ROOM:
- Located in northwest island of Sparkling Skylands
- Unlocked by completing the Pokedex

IS THERE A SECRET ENDING?
- No - only one ending exists
- Game continues as postgame after credits

title: How to Complete the Mysterious Mural
content:
Guide to completing the Mysterious Mural secret puzzle in Pokemon Pokopia.

WHAT IS THE MYSTERIOUS MURAL?
- An Unown puzzle requiring 27 Mysterious Slates
- Hidden underground room in Withered Wasteland
- Reward: Befriend mythical Pokemon MEW

HOW TO FIND THE MURAL:

1. Go northeast of the Pokemon Center in Withered Wasteland
2. Break the wall to find a hidden area
3. Break the wall to the left where you see chiseled blocks
4. This uncovers stairs going down
5. Follow stairs to the Mysterious Mural

HOW TO COMPLETE:

1. Collect all 27 Mysterious Slates
2. Attach each slate to the correct Unown symbol on the mural
3. Match according to corresponding Unown Alphabet on back of each slate
4. Attach ALL 27 slates correctly

REWARD: Mew appears and can be befriended!
Note: Give Mew a new home since it won't have a habitat when it spawns.

HOW TO GET MYSTERIOUS SLATES:

- Find blocks with glowing spots
- Break them with Rock Smash
- Have a chance to drop Mysterious Slates

USING DOWSING MACHINE TO FIND SLATES:

1. Get a Pokemon with Search specialty to follow you
2. Press A to use the Dowsing Machine
3. Pokemon points toward direction of glowing spots

title: How Long to Beat Pokemon Pokopia
content:
Game length information for Pokemon Pokopia.

ESTIMATED GAME LENGTH:

Main Story (Credits): 20-40 hours
- Per director Takuto Edagawa in interviews
- Note: Game is a life sim, not designed to rush to credits

100% Completion: ~100 hours (estimated)
- Discover all 300+ Pokemon
- Build all habitats
- Collect all recipes and craft all items
- Gather all collectibles (relics, fossils, human records)

WHY IT TAKES LONGER:

Time-Based Mechanics:
- Construction tied to real-world time
- Some Pokemon appear only at specific times of day
- Building construction can take minutes to hours

Life Sim Design:
- Designed to play slowly at your own pace
- Cozy game: Can sink hundreds of hours

GAME DESIGN NOTE:
Pokopia is designed as a game where you set your own pace. There's no rush to reach the credits - the journey of building your town is the experience.

title: All Missables and One-Time Events
content:
List of permanent choices and missable content in Pokemon Pokopia.

TRUE ONE-TIME EVENTS (Cannot be changed):

1. Character Name
- When you start, you choose your character's name
- This CANNOT be changed later
- Choose wisely!

2. Starting Island Layout
- All playthroughs begin on the same island with same layout
- Required for tutorial and initial setup
- Cannot be changed

NOT ACTUALLY MISSABLE:

Character Appearance:
- Can be changed after starting!
- Use a mirror to customize appearance whenever you want
- Purchase mirror from PC Shop or find in ruins

REPEATABLE EVENTS:

Most events in Pokopia can be repeated or redone:
- Daily activities reset every day at 5 AM
- Events can be revisited during their active period
- Time traveling lets you replay days

TIPS FOR MISSABLES:
- Choose your character name carefully at game start
- Everything else in appearance can be changed later via mirror

title: Should You Go to Bleak Beach or Rocky Ridges First?
content:
Guide to choosing which area to visit first: Bleak Beach or Rocky Ridges.

SHORT ANSWER:
You can develop BOTH areas simultaneously! No wrong choice.

HOW TO UNLOCK BOTH AREAS:
- Increase Trainer Rank to Great
- Complete the Yawn Up a Storm! request in Withered Wasteland
- Meet Kyogre to gain Great Trainer Rank
- Green gates open - use Pokedex scan to pass through

GATE LOCATIONS:
- Bleak Beach Gate: East of Withered Wasteland (near cliffs and small vegetable field)
- Rocky Ridges Gate: West of area (dark cave by the sea)

BENEFITS OF GOING TO ROCKY RIDGES FIRST:

1. Earlier access to Rollout move
- Learn from Graveler
- Move faster and break blocks faster

2. Earlier access to Smelting Furnace
- Turn ore items into ingots
- Essential for later crafting

BENEFITS OF GOING TO BLEAK BEACH FIRST:

1. Earlier access to Iron Ore
- Obtained by destroying debris or giving Trubbish nonburnable garbage
- Needed for Utility Poles (connect to power sources for electricity)
- Needed for Iron Ingots (requires Smelting Furnace)

FINAL RECOMMENDATION:
Both areas can be developed simultaneously - explore whichever appeals to you more. The game doesn't punish you for choosing either order.

title: How to Raise Humidity
content:
Guide to raising and managing humidity in Pokemon Pokopia.

WHAT HUMIDITY DOES:
- Affects Pokemon comfort levels for water-loving Pokemon
- Required for Yawn Up a Storm! request (100 humidity)
- Check via Slowpoke's Yawn specialty

HOW TO CHECK HUMIDITY:
- Talk to Slowpoke (only Yawn specialty Pokemon)
- Ask them to Yawn
- Gives humidity percentage for the entire biome (not individual habitats)

HOW TO RAISE HUMIDITY:

1. Rain Dance Site (fastest after story)
- Build Rain Dance Site habitat
- Blue Castform weather charms = rain form
- Bring Water specialty Pokemon (Squirtle) to eat offering
- Rain waters all exposed dry plants = big humidity boost

2. Bodies of Water
- Use Suck move (from Piplup) to move water tiles
- Use Rock Smash on blocks with bubbles to create springs
- Expand existing water bodies with Rock Smash
- Use Water Gun on Water Basin to create indoor water source

3. Horsea Fountains (main game method)
- Use Rock Smash on shiny rock northwest of Pokemon Center
- Place Horsea Fountain in water
- Break more rocks to expand water area for second fountain

4. Vegetation
- Flowers, trees, grass increase habitat humidity
- Must be watered (not dry/withered)
- Use Water Gun to water plants

5. Outdoor Furniture
- Sprinklers and fountains spread water
- Must be in/near water to work

HUMIDITY NOTE:
- Humidity stuck at 71 during Yawn Up a Storm? Must talk to involved Pokemon and progress quest
- Other methods won't increase humidity past this point until story progresses

HUMID HABITAT POKEMON (prefer humid environments):
- Squirtle line, Slowpoke line, Gyarados, Lapras
- Lotad line, Goomy line, various Water types
- These Pokemon's comfort levels increase with humid habitats

title: How to Change the Weather
content:
Guide to changing weather conditions in Pokemon Pokopia.

WEATHER TYPES:
- Sunny
- Rainy
- Cloudy (natural, cannot be manually triggered)

HOW TO CHANGE TO SUNNY:

1. Build a Sunny Day Site (Castform charms showing Sun form)
   - Inspect Castform Weather Charms near them to flip/change colors
   - Orange charms = Sunny Day Site
2. Place food offering on wooden plate
3. Find a Burn specialty Pokemon (e.g., Charmander)
4. Lead them to the Sunny Day Site
5. Let them eat the offering
6. Wait a short while - weather changes to Sunny

HOW TO CHANGE TO RAINY:

1. Build a Rain Dance Site (Castform charms showing Rain form)
   - Blue charms = Rain Dance Site
2. Place food offering on wooden plate
3. Find a Water specialty Pokemon (e.g., Squirtle)
4. Lead them to the Rain Dance Site
5. Let them eat the offering
6. Wait a short while - weather changes to Rainy

WEATHER EFFECTS:

Rain:
- Hydrates dry land and vegetation
- Increases humidity
- Summons Lugia over the island

Sunny:
- Dry, warm conditions
- Summons Ho-Oh over the island

WEATHER-EXCLUSIVE POKEMON:

Sunny only: Cacnea

Rainy only:
- Goomy / Sliggoo / Goodra
- Azumarill

LEGENDARY FEATHERS:
- Ho-Oh appears during Sunny Days → Rainbow Feather
- Lugia appears during Rainy Days → Silver Feather
- These feathers can be traded with Gimmighoul

title: How to Plant Seeds and Grow Crops
content:
Complete guide to farming crops in Pokemon Pokopia.

HOW TO PLANT:

Step 1: Till the Field
- Use Rototiller while in grass or dirt
- Creates crop fields that can be planted in
- Learn Rototiller from Drilbur by accepting their request

Step 2: Select and Plant Seeds
- Select seeds from inventory
- Hold them, aim at tilled field
- Press A to plant

Step 3: Water if Needed
- Some crops need Water Gun to grow
- Others grow on their own
- Darker color indicates properly watered crop

GROWING TIPS:

Speed Up Growth:
- Use Grow specialty Pokemon (like Bulbasaur)
- Have them follow you to planting site
- They automatically grow plants

Irrigation Methods:
- Place plants near water source (constantly watered by stream)
- Good location: Near Drilbur's area (left side of Pokemon Center, Withered Wasteland)
- Water specialty Pokemon can water plants if basin is filled
- Fill basin with Water Gun

HARVESTING:

Individual Harvest:
- Press Y to pick up crops when fully grown
- One at a time

Bulk Harvest (BEST METHOD):
- Use Cut move aimed at crop block
- Press ZR to use Cut on crops
- Hold Y to inhale all cut crops at once
- Power dishes let you cut wider fields!

FINDING SEEDS:

1. Buy from Pokemon Center Shop
- Limited stock, refreshes daily

2. Cut flowers and trees
- Drop seeds when cut

3. Gifts from Pokemon
- Give gifts, complete requests, build habitats
- Pokemon may give seeds as rewards

IMPORTANT CROPS:
- Beans (Withered Wasteland): Simple Hamburger Steak, Party curry
- Tomatoes (Bleak Beach): Various recipes
- Wheat (Rocky Ridges): Simple Bread, Party curry
- Various vegetables: Team Initiation Challenge requirements

CROPS AND VEGETABLES:
- Team Initiation Challenge requires: 10 Beans, 10 Tomatoes, 10 Wheat
- Eeveelutions task: Grow 50 vegetables at Palette Town

title: List of All Fossil Pokemon
content:
All Fossil Pokemon in Pokemon Pokopia and how to befriend them.

FOSSIL POKEMON LIST:

#271 Aerodactyl (Rock/Flying) - Wing Fossil Display - Fly specialty
#272 Cranidos (Rock) - Skull Fossil Display - Crush specialty
#273 Rampardos (Rock) - Headbutt Fossil Display - Litter + Crush specialty
#274 Shieldon (Rock/Steel) - Armor Fossil Display - Crush specialty
#275 Bastiodon (Rock/Steel) - Shield Fossil Display - Litter + Crush specialty
#276 Tyrunt (Rock/Dragon) - Jaw Fossil Display - Crush specialty
#277 Tyrantrum (Rock/Dragon) - Despot Fossil Display - Litter + Crush specialty
#278 Amaura (Rock/Ice) - Sail Fossil Display - Crush specialty
#279 Aurorus (Rock/Ice) - Tundra Fossil Display - Litter + Crush specialty

HOW TO SPAWN FOSSIL POKEMON:

Place Fossils on Exhibition Stands:
- Base forms (except Aerodactyl): 1 Fossil
- Other forms: Multiple fossil parts required

FOSSIL LOCATIONS BY BIOME:

Withered Wasteland:
- Armor Fossil (1 piece)
- Shield Fossil (head, body, tail = 3 pieces)
- Total: 4 fossils needed
- Unlocks: Shieldon + Bastiodon

Bleak Beach:
- Sail Fossil (1 piece)
- Tundra Fossil (head, body, tail = 3 pieces)
- Total: 4 fossils needed
- Unlocks: Amaura + Aurorus

Rocky Ridges:
- Jaw Fossil (1 piece)
- Wing Fossil (head, right wing, left wing, body, tail = 5 pieces)
- Despot Fossil (head, body, legs, tail = 4 pieces)
- Total: 10 fossils needed
- Unlocks: Aerodactyl, Tyrunt, Tyrantrum

Sparkling Skylands:
- Skull Fossil (1 piece)
- Headbutt Fossil (head, body, tail = 3 pieces)
- Total: 4 fossils needed
- Unlocks: Cranidos + Rampardos

SPECIFIC FOSSIL HABITATS:

Armor Fossil Display: Exhibition Stand x1 + Armor Fossil x1 → Shieldon
Shield Fossil Display: Exhibition Stand x2 + Shield Fossil Head + Body + Tail → Bastiodon
Jaw Fossil Display: Exhibition Stand x1 + Jaw Fossil x1 → Tyrunt
Wing Fossil Display: Exhibition Stand x1 + Wing Fossil Head + Right Wing + Left Wing + Body + Tail → Aerodactyl
Despot Fossil Display: Exhibition Stand x1 + Despot Fossil Head + Body + Legs + Tail → Tyrantrum

Tundra Fossil Display: Exhibition Stand x1 + Tundra Fossil Head + Body + Tail → Aurorus

HOW TO FIND FOSSILS:
1. Look for Glowing rocks
2. Use Rock Smash to break them
3. Pick up dropped items (use Inhale or Y)
4. Use Dowsing Machine + Search specialty Pokemon to locate Glowing rocks more easily
5. Use Magnet Rise (postgame) to fly and scout surface glowing rocks
6. Time travel to respawn Glowing rocks after clearing all in an area

title: How to Go to the S.S. Anne
content:
Guide to reaching the S.S. Anne in Pokemon Pokopia.

WHERE IS S.S. ANNE:
- Located south of Bleak Beach
- The ruined luxury cruise ship from original Kanto games

HOW TO ACCESS:

Method 1 - Swim (Recommended):
1. Unlock Great Trainer Rank
2. Go to Bleak Beach
3. Befriend Lapras by creating Tropical Seaside habitat
4. Learn Surf ability from Lapras
5. Use Surf to swim south to S.S. Anne

Method 2 - Bridge:
1. Go near the cruise ship area
2. Find the broken bridge beside S.S. Anne
3. Manually restore the bridge with blocks
4. Note: Must complete Bleak Beach story first for this to progress/unlock Happiny

WHAT TO DO AT S.S. ANNE:

Meet Happiny:
- Located inside S.S. Anne
- Only appears after completing Bleak Beach story
- Talk to befriend and register in Pokedex

After completing Bleak Beach story:
- Trubbish gives you a request to check out S.S. Anne

LORE NOTE:
- S.S. Anne is severely ruined due to the apocalypse
- In original games, it docked at Vermillion City (likely = Bleak Beach)
- Required S.S. Ticket from Bill to access in original games

title: How to Get Electricity to Power Your Island
content:
Complete guide to electricity system in Pokemon Pokopia.

POWER SOURCES:

Windmill:
- Energy: 10 units (lower elevation), 20 units (higher elevation)
- How to power: Constantly generates power
- Find in overworld or buy building kit from PC Shop

Waterwheel:
- Energy: 20 units
- How to power: Have running water under the wheel
- Water must flow beneath the wheel

Furnace:
- Energy: 30 units
- How to power: Put burnable objects inside (Lumber, Sturdy Sticks)
- Each item of energy typically powers one item

HOW TO DISTRIBUTE POWER:

Utility Poles:
- Place poles to create line from power generator to items
- Poles must be close enough to connect (lit up = connected)
- Each utility pole can power up to 20 items
- Recipe: Found on island near southwest corner of Bleak Beach (near Pokeball by furnace)
- Can place underground to reduce clutter

TIPS:
- Can relocate/demolish power sources with kits (buy from PC Shop)
- Need Bulldoze specialty Pokemon + 1 additional Pokemon to use kits

GENERATE SPECIALTY (Temporary):
- Find Pokemon with Generate specialty
- Ask them to follow you
- Lead to item needing power
- Item loses power when picked up - needs re-powering after placement

WHAT ELECTRICITY DOES:

1. Powers Lights
- Lamps, streetlights, spotlights need electricity
- Creates bright habitats for Pokemon that like brightness

2. Powers Habitat Items
- Some habitats require powered items to spawn Pokemon

3. Powers Vending Machines
- One random drink per day when powered
- Drinks create water/lava or used for cooking/crafting

4. Peakychu's Illuminate Specialty
- Need charging station connected to generators via utility poles
- Bring Peakychu to charging station to charge up
- Then she can illuminate the whole town

title: How to Rebuild the Pokemon Center
content:
Complete guide to rebuilding Pokemon Centers in each biome.

HOW TO UNLOCK:
1. Raise Environment Level to 3 in the biome
2. Professor Tangrowth gives important request

HOW TO REBUILD:
1. Increase Environment Level to 3
2. Purchase Pokemon Center Rebuilding Kit from PC Shop (1000 Life Coins)
3. Kit is placed automatically in front of Pokemon Center
4. Stand near kit to deposit required materials
5. Bring required Pokemon with needed specialties
6. Wait until next real-world day (or change system clock)

MATERIALS REQUIRED BY BIOME:

Withered Wasteland:
- Lumber x20, Stone x20, Leaf x20, Vine Rope x20
- Specialties needed: Bulldoze, Build, Chop
- Pokemon needed: 5

Bleak Beach:
- Twine x10, Brick x20, Sea Glass Fragments x10, Iron Ore x5
- Specialties needed: Bulldoze, Build, Water
- Pokemon needed: 5

Rocky Ridges:
- Stone x25, Copper Ingot x25, Iron Ingot x25, Crystal Fragment x5
- Specialties needed: Bulldoze, Build, Crush
- Pokemon needed: 5

Sparkling Skylands:
- Pokemetal x25, Concrete x25, Glass x25, Gold Ingot x25
- Specialties needed: Bulldoze, Build, Gather
- Pokemon needed: 5

Palette Town:
- Lumber x20, Stone x20, Leaf x20, Vine Rope x20
- Specialties needed: Bulldoze, Build, Chop
- Pokemon needed: 5

POKEMON CENTER FEATURES:

Healing Machine:
- Right side of interior
- Fully restore PP once per day
- Essential for using moves

3D Printer:
- Left side of interior
- Duplicates items with Reference Photos
- Costs Pokemetal to use
- Can copy items from Dream Islands and other players' worlds

Trading:
- Trade specialty Pokemon in Pokemon Center
- Exchange items of similar or higher value

Remodeling:
- At Environment Level 10, unlock that biome's Pokemon Center design kit
- Buy kit for 1000 Life Coins
- Use on another biome's Pokemon Center to change design
- Must have fully rebuilt Pokemon Center first

title: Best Pokemon Specialty to Follow You Around
content:
Tier ranking for the best Pokemon specialties to have following you.

BEST SPECIALTIES TO HAVE FOLLOW YOU:

S-Tier (Most Useful):
- Search: Find valuable items with Dowsing Machine
- DJ: Play music while exploring

A-Tier (Very Useful):
- Grow: Speed up plant growth automatically
- Burn: Light fires and smelt automatically
- Gather: Collect dropped materials
- Water: Water plants automatically
- Generate: Power machines temporarily

B-Tier (Situationally Useful):
- Build: Help construct buildings
- Bulldoze: Help demolish structures
- Fly: Fast travel to Pokemon locations
- Hype: Improve mood with music
- Teleport: Fast travel to Pokemon locations

BEST OVERALL: Gulpin (Storage specialty)
- Gives more inventory space
- More accessible in early game
- Gulpin and Swalot are the only Storage specialty Pokemon
- Found in Tantalizing Dining Set and Dinner Table Surprise habitats

SEARCH SPECIALTY:
- Use Dowsing Machine to find Lost Relics, CDs, Mysterious Slates, Dolls
- Must have Dowsing Machine item in inventory

DJ SPECIALTY (DJ Rotom):
- Plays music CDs while you explore
- Stores up to 5 CDs
- Plays in order (left to right), loops back to first CD
- Great for mood/ambiance

FLY AND TELEPORT:
- Limited: Only fast travel to specific Pokemon (not locations)
- Cannot fly freely or view island from above

USING SPECIALTIES:
- Have Pokemon follow you
- Lead to item/location needing their specialty
- They automatically perform their task when nearby
- Max 5 Pokemon can follow you at once

title: How to Have Pokemon Follow You
content:
Guide to having Pokemon follow you in Pokemon Pokopia.

HOW TO GET POKEMON TO FOLLOW YOU:

Method 1: Talk to Pokemon
- Walk up to Pokemon
- Talk to them
- Select 'Follow me!'
- They'll start following you

Method 2: Press Up on D-Pad
- Approach Pokemon
- Press Up button on controller
- Both Ditto and Pokemon react
- Pokemon starts following

IF POKEMON WON'T FOLLOW:
- They may be hungry
- Give them food first
- Then ask again

HOW TO STOP FOLLOWING:
- Hold the Up button
- Pokemon will part ways

MAXIMUM POKEMON FOLLOWING:
- Up to 5 Pokemon at once
- Swap out by dismissing some first

BEST POKEMON TO BRING (Gulpin):
- Storage specialty = more inventory space
- Very accessible early in game
- Found in Tantalizing Dining Set habitat

WHAT TO DO WITH FOLLOWING POKEMON:

1. Use Their Specialties
- Lead to item/location
- They automatically do their job
- Example: Charmander (Burn) near campfire = lights it

2. Help With Construction
- Bring to construction site
- Interact with kit to see requirements
- Required Pokemon must be present

3. Move to New Home
- Build house
- Lead Pokemon there
- Ask them to move in (can't dislike any items in house)

4. Move Between Areas
- Walk to gate with Pokemon following
- OR use Return Home function to fast travel (Pokemon teleports with you)

5. Complete Requests
- Some requests need you to bring Pokemon somewhere

title: How to Fast Travel
content:
Guide to fast traveling between areas in Pokemon Pokopia.

HOW TO FAST TRAVEL:

Method 1: Return Home
- Open Pokedex
- Select Return Home
- Choose which area's home to travel to
- Need a house in that area (claimed with Ditto Flag)
- Without a home: Teleports to nearest safe location

Method 2: Fly or Teleport Specialty Pokemon
- Use Pokedex to search for a specific Pokemon
- Talk to a Pokemon with Fly or Teleport specialty
- They transport you to the searched Pokemon's location
- Only travels to specific Pokemon, not general locations

Method 3: Link Play (Multiplayer)
- Access Link Play at Pokemon Center PC
- Visit other player islands
- Create Cloud Islands
- Available at Environment Level 2

FAST TRAVEL TIPS:

Unlock by Completing Requests:
- First new area available after Yawn Up a Storm! request
- Completing request upgrades rank to Great Trainer
- Gate to East of Pokemon Center opens

Use Return Home to Get Unstuck:
- If stuck in water or surrounded by hazards
- Return Home gets you to safety

Inventory Management:
- Fast travel home when inventory is full
- Great for long exploration sessions

WHEN POKEMON FOLLOW YOU:
- Using Return Home takes the following Pokemon with you
- They teleport to your home location too

title: How to Use the Smelting Furnace
content:
Complete guide to the Smelting Furnace in Pokemon Pokopia.

HOW TO USE SMELTING FURNACE:

1. Place material in the furnace (only one type at a time)
2. Bring a Pokemon with Burn specialty close to it
3. Wait for material to process
4. Pick up processed product (sparkles = ready to collect)

AUTOMATION TIP: Place Smelting Furnace inside/near a Burn specialty Pokemon's house - they'll automatically smelt when home!

WHAT YOU CAN SMELT:

Iron Ore → Iron Ingot
Copper Ore → Copper Ingot
Gold Ore → Gold Ingot
Beach Sand → Glass
Sandstone → Glass
Volcanic Ash → Glass
Pokemetal Fragment → Pokemetal
Rare Pokemetal Fragment → Rare Pokemetal

HOW TO UNLOCK:

1. Complete Machoke's "Cut Down that Fence" request
2. Head to cave left of Pokemon Center (Rocky Ridges)
3. Follow Machoke until you reach a metal fence
4. Eat Bread (powered-up Cut), use Cut to remove fence
5. Head further in to clearing with Workbench
6. Smelting Furnace is against wall on the left

(Clearing metal fence = part of Cut Down that Fence request, which you get from Machoke after freeing Chef Dente)

BEST POKEMON FOR SMELTING: Magmar
- Easily create habitat near Smelting Furnace
- Put Wheelbarrow and Excavation Tools next to furnace (2x2 space)
- Creates Digging and Burning habitat for Magmar

CRAFTING MORE FURNACES:
- Use Smelting Furnace once to unlock the recipe
- Craft Smelting Furnaces at any area

title: Time Travel Guide: How to Change the Time of Day
content:
Complete guide to time traveling in Pokemon Pokopia.

HOW TO TIME TRAVEL FORWARD:

1. Save your game
2. Select System Settings from HOME screen (no need to close game)
3. Go to Date and Time under System menu
4. Turn OFF 'Synchronize Clock Using the Internet'
5. Manually adjust Date and Time to desired future date
6. Start Pokemon Pokopia

Note: A new day starts at 5 AM. Travel to 5 AM to start the next day.

HOW TO TIME TRAVEL BACKWARD:

1. Save your game
2. Select System Settings from HOME screen
3. Go to Date and Time under System menu
4. Turn OFF 'Synchronize Clock Using the Internet'
5. Adjust to a date BEFORE your desired date
6. Open Pokemon Pokopia briefly to process the change
7. Save and close
8. Go back to settings and time travel FORWARD to intended date

WHY BACKWARD IS DIFFERENT:
- Going backwards won't reverse your progress
- You keep all Pokemon and story progress
- Must travel to date before, then forward to buffer/process the change
- This lets you replay missed events

TIME OF DAY SCHEDULE:

Morning: 6 AM to 9 AM
Day: 9 AM to 5 PM
Dusk: 5 PM to 7 PM
Night: 7 PM to 6 AM

NEW DAY STARTS: 5 AM

WHEN TIME IS FIXED:
- During tutorial at game start
- Time syncs with real world after Ditto falls asleep

NO PENALTIES:
- No penalties for time traveling
- Play normally after time skip
- Can go backward without losing progress

USES FOR TIME TRAVEL:

1. Spawn Time-Exclusive Pokemon
2. Speed up construction (buildings take up to a day)
3. Replay missed events
4. Skip to specific times of day

NIGHT-EXCLUSIVE POKEMON:
Oddish, Venonat, Venomoth, Hoothoot, Noctowl, Cacturne, Zubat, Golbat, Crobat, Gastly, Haunter, Gengar, Cleffa, Clefairy, Clefable, Murkrow, Honchkrow, Misdreavus, Mismagius, Duskull, Dusclops, Dusknoir, Umbreon

Note: No Pokemon appear to be exclusive to daytime.

title: How to Break Glowing Spots
content:
Guide to breaking glowing spots and rocks in Pokemon Pokopia.

HOW TO BREAK GLOWING SPOTS:

If Glowing Spot is in front of you:
- Use Rock Smash move to destroy the rock

If Glowing Spot is under you:
- Position camera to face the ground
- Use Rock Smash while aiming downward

HOW TO GET ROCK SMASH:
- Learn from Hitmonchan
- Complete Hitmonchan's requests
- Befriend Hitmonchan

ITEMS DROPPED BY GLOWING SPOTS:

1. Ancient Artifacts
- Lost Relics (bring to Professor Tangrowth for appraisal)
- Fossils (for Exhibition Stands)
- WARNING: Lost Relics can detonate! Use Water Gun to defuse

2. CDs
- Change town background music
- Must meet DJ Rotom first to use

3. Mysterious Slates
- Attach to Mysterious Mural in Withered Wasteland
- Match according to Unknown Alphabet on back
- 27 Slates total = surprise when complete

ABOUT GLOWING SPOTS:
- Scattered around all biomes
- Considered rare spawns
- Limited number spawn each day
- Sometimes additional glowing rocks appear
- Use time travel to respawn after clearing area

TIPS:
- Bring Water Gun ready for detonating relics
- Visit all biomes daily to maximize finds
- Use Search specialty Pokemon + Dowsing Machine to find more easily

title: Dowsing Machine Guide: All Pokemon with Search Specialty
content:
Complete guide to the Dowsing Machine and Search specialty in Pokemon Pokopia.

HOW TO USE DOWSING MACHINE:

1. Find a Pokemon with Search Specialty (like Pidgey)
2. Equip Dowsing Machine from Inventory
3. Walk around while holding it
4. Watch the Pokemon - they indicate treasure nearby
5. Use Rock Smash to dig and unveil treasure

ITEMS FOUND WITH DOWSING MACHINE:

1. Ancient Artifacts
- Lost Relics (appraise with Professor Tangrowth)
- Fossils (for Exhibition Stands)
- WARNING: Lost Relics can detonate - use Water Gun to defuse

2. CDs
- Change town background music
- Must meet DJ Rotom first

3. Mysterious Slates
- Attach to Mysterious Mural in Withered Wasteland
- Match to Unknown Alphabet on back
- 27 Slates = surprise when complete

4. Dolls
- Present to Drifloon to go to Dream Islands
- Each doll = specific island with unique materials

HOW TO GET DOWSING MACHINE:
- Complete 'Check what's up there' request
- Request from Hitmonchan (left side of Withered Wasteland)

CRAFTING DOWSING MACHINE:
- Pokemetal x1
- Craft at Workbench

BEST SEARCH POKEMON TO BEFRIEND:

Pidgey (Easiest):
- Common rarity
- Attracted to Pretty Flower Bed habitat

Drilbur:
- Head east from Withered Wasteland
- Requires 8 Vegetable Fields for habitat

ALL SEARCH SPECIALTY POKEMON:
See specialties list - Search is one of 30 specialties

title: 3D Printer Guide: Best Things to Copy
content:
Complete guide to the 3D Printer in Pokemon Pokopia.

HOW TO USE 3D PRINTER:

1. Take reference photo of object you want to duplicate
2. Submit photo to 3D Printer (in Pokemon Center)
3. Set quantity desired
4. Exchange required Pokemetal
5. Receive item

COSTS:
- Minimum: 2 Pokemetal for 1-block furniture
- Larger objects = more Pokemetal
- Some items require Rare Pokemetal (like Horsea Fountain)

HOW TO TAKE REFERENCE PHOTOS:
- Press Y while using Camera
- Changes Camera Mode for reference photos
- You get 20 Album Slots for references
- Delete photos to make space for more

BEST THINGS TO COPY:

1. Rare Habitat Items
- Items needed for rare habitats (like Gravestone)
- Print copies if recipe is hard to find

2. Unique Furniture Patterns
- Furniture with unique prints/styles
- Items you like but don't have resources for

HOW TO UNLOCK:
- Finish rebuilding Pokemon Center
- Accessible in any Pokemon Center location
- Unlimited uses per day

TIPS:
- Great for duplicating items from Dream Islands
- Can copy items from other players' worlds
- Use for hard-to-craft or hard-to-find items

title: How to Complete the Mysterious Mural
content:
Complete guide to the Mysterious Mural and Mysterious Slates in Pokemon Pokopia.

WHAT IS THE MYSTERIOUS MURAL:
- Unown puzzle in Withered Wasteland
- Requires piecing together 27 Mysterious Slates
- Match slates to Unown Alphabet symbols on back
- Reward: Mythical Pokemon Mew

MYSTERIOUS MURAL LOCATION:
1. Go northeast of Pokemon Center in Withered Wasteland
2. Break the wall to find hidden area
3. Break wall to the left where you see chiseled blocks
4. This uncovers stairs going down to the Mural

HOW TO GET MYSTERIOUS SLATES:

1. Find Glowing Spots
- Blocks with glowing spots
- Break them for chance to drop Slates

2. Use Dowsing Machine
- Have Search specialty Pokemon follow you
- Press A to use Dowsing Machine
- Pokemon points toward glowing spots

3. Use Rock Smash
- Break glowing rocks to collect Slates

REWARD FOR COMPLETING MURAL:
- Summons Mythical Pokemon Mew
- Befriend Mew and add to Pokedex
- Give Mew a new home (has no habitat when spawned)

TOTAL SLATES NEEDED: 27

TIPS:
- Time travel to respawn glowing spots after clearing area
- Visit all biomes daily to maximize slate finds
- Bring Search Pokemon + Dowsing Machine for efficiency

title: Stamp Rally Guide
content:
Complete guide to the Stamp Rally in Pokemon Pokopia.

HOW TO PARTICIPATE:
- Unlock PC first (any Pokemon Center)
- You don't need to repair the area's Pokemon Center
- Get stamp cards every Friday

STAMP CARD REQUIREMENTS:
- Need 5 stamps per week
- Fill all slots during stamp period (1 week)
- Redemption period = week after stamp period ends

HOW TO GET STAMPS:

1. Your Own World PCs
- Check daily at any PC
- Same stamp available at all your PCs each day

2. Friend's World (Multiplayer)
- Different stamp from your world
- Get multiple stamps in one day this way

3. Cloud Island PCs
- Different Cloud Islands can have different stamps
- Create your own or join friend's via Link Code

STAMP RARITY & REWARDS:

Common (plain circle border, basic Pokemon):
- Worth 50 Life Coins

Uncommon (wavy border, evolved Pokemon):
- Worth 100 Life Coins

Rare (wavy border, Legendary Pokemon):
- Worth 200 Life Coins

Mythical (special border, shiny film):
- Worth 1000 Life Coins

BONUS REWARD:
- Complete Stamp Card = bonus 1,500 Life Coins

TIPS:
- Prioritize rare stamps for more coins
- Swap identical stamps for unique stamps in each slot
- Visit other islands to fill card faster
- Time travel if you can't play daily
- You can replace existing stamps with better ones

title: Money Farming Guide: How to Earn Life Coins
content:
HOW TO EARN LIFE COINS:

METHOD 1: Complete Challenges (Main Method)
1. Unlock PC in Withered Wasteland
2. Open PC at Pokemon Center
3. Check available Challenges
4. Complete tasks (habitats, resources, find Pokemon)
5. Return to PC to claim rewards
6. Daily Challenges refresh at 5:00 AM

CHALLENGE TASKS INCLUDE:
- Making habitats
- Gathering resources
- Interacting with Pokemon
- Finding new Pokemon

METHOD 2: Stamp Rally
1. Get stamp card every Friday
2. Collect 5 stamps throughout the week
3. Fill card during stamp period
4. Redeem during redemption period
5. Rare stamps = more Life Coins

WAYS TO UNLOCK MORE CHALLENGES:
- Increase Environment Level
- Befriend new Pokemon
- Build houses
- Complete requests
- Increase Pokemon comfort levels

WHAT LIFE COINS ARE USED FOR:
- Buy furniture from Shop
- Buy recipes
- Buy seeds
- Buy building kits
- Essential for progression

STAMP REWARD VALUES:
- Common stamps: 50 Life Coins
- Uncommon stamps: 100 Life Coins
- Rare stamps: 200 Life Coins
- Mythical stamps: 1000 Life Coins
- Complete card bonus: 1500 Life Coins

TIPS:
- Claim challenges at PC (not automatic)
- Visit other islands for more stamps
- Time travel to speed up stamp collection
- Higher Environment Level = more challenges
